mission.title: Install and Configure Chaos Mesh on Kubernetes
mission.description: Chaos Mesh is an open source cloud-native Chaos Engineering platform that allows you to simulate various faults in Kubernetes environments. Installing Chaos Mesh enables you to orchestrate chaos experiments, helping to identify potential issues in your applications and improve their resilience.

steps.title: Add the Chaos Mesh Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Chaos Mesh Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add chaos-mesh https://charts.chaos-mesh.org
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Chaos Mesh using Helm
steps.description:
Install Chaos Mesh with the specified version and create a namespace for it:
```bash
helm install chaos-mesh chaos-mesh/chaos-mesh --version v2.8.1 --namespace chaos-mesh --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Chaos Mesh pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace chaos-mesh
```

steps.title: Access the Chaos Dashboard
steps.description:
Port-forward the Chaos Dashboard service to access it locally:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/chaos-dashboard -n chaos-mesh 2333:2333
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Chaos Mesh will have all pods running in the 'chaos-mesh' namespace, and you will be able to access the Chaos Dashboard at http://localhost:2333. You can now start creating and managing chaos experiments.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add chaos-mesh https://charts.chaos-mesh.org
helm repo update
helm install chaos-mesh chaos-mesh/chaos-mesh --version v2.8.1 --namespace chaos-mesh --create-namespace

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Chaos Mesh release from your Kubernetes cluster using Helm. This will remove all associated resources except for CRDs and persistent volumes.
```bash
helm uninstall chaos-mesh --namespace chaos-mesh
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Remove the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) associated with Chaos Mesh. This ensures that all remnants of the installation are cleaned up.
```bash
kubectl delete crd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=chaos-mesh
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the namespace used for Chaos Mesh and verify that it has been removed successfully.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace chaos-mesh
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current state of your Chaos Mesh installation to avoid data loss.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace chaos-mesh -o yaml > chaos-mesh-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade the Chaos Mesh installation to the latest version.
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade chaos-mesh chaos-mesh/chaos-mesh --version v2.9.0 --namespace chaos-mesh
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check if the Chaos Mesh pods are running the new version successfully after the upgrade.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace chaos-mesh
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If you see that the Chaos Mesh pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for the pod to diagnose the issue.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace chaos-mesh
```

troubleshooting.title: Chaos Dashboard not accessible
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Chaos Dashboard, ensure that the port-forward command is running and check the service status.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace chaos-mesh
```

troubleshooting.title: RBAC issues preventing access
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter permission denied errors, verify that the RBAC configuration is correctly applied and the necessary roles are assigned.
```bash
kubectl get clusterrolebindings | grep chaos-mesh
```

troubleshooting.title: Resource limits causing pod evictions
troubleshooting.description:
If pods are being evicted due to resource limits, check the resource requests and limits set for the Chaos Mesh deployment.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace chaos-mesh
```

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
